title: Turkish-Style Kuru Fasulye
description: A hearty Turkish white bean stew made with kuru beans, onions, and spices.
instructions: Rinse the dried beans and soak them in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soaked beans, then transfer them to a large pot.
Add the chopped onion, Turkish red pepper (if using), and garlic to the pot.
Pour in enough water to cover the beans and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour, or until the beans are tender.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Heat the olive oil in a pan over medium heat.
Sauté the beans and onion mixture for 10-15 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together.
Serve the Turkish-style kuru fasulye hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
